title: Kumbharia Jain temples
tagline: Kumbharia Jain temples — spiritual town of Gujarat

overview.short: The Kumbharia Jain temples is a group of five Jain temples in the Kumbhariya, Banaskantha district in Gujarat, India.
overview.description: The Kumbharia Jain temples is a group of five Jain temples in the Kumbhariya, Banaskantha district in Gujarat, India. Constructed from 1062 to 1231 CE during the reign of the Chaulukya dynasty, they are noted for their elaborate architecture.

topPlaces.name: Ambaji Mata Temple
topPlaces.description: Ambaji Mata Temple (also known as Arasuri Amba Temple) is a prominent Hindu temple in the town of Ambaji in Banaskantha district, Gujarat, India. Revered as one of the 51 Shakta pithas, the temple is traditionally believed to enshrine the heart of the goddess Sati.

topPlaces.name: Ambaji
topPlaces.description: Ambaji (Ambājī) is a census town in Banaskantha district in the state of Gujarat, India.

topPlaces.name: Ambaji ropeway
topPlaces.description: Ambaji ropeway is a ropeway on Gabbar hill in Ambaji, Banaskantha district, Gujarat, India. It was opened in 1998.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kumbharia Jain temples?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kumbharia Jain temples is Oct – Mar. Summers are 28–44°C and winters are 8–25°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kumbharia Jain temples?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Maharana Pratap Airport, Udaipur (110 km) and the nearest railway station is Udaipur City (89 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kumbharia Jain temples?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kumbharia Jain temples at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kumbharia Jain temples?
faq.a: Stays in Kumbharia Jain temples start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kumbharia Jain temples?
faq.a: The top attractions are Ambaji Mata Temple, Ambaji, Ambaji ropeway.
